title: Biotech ETFs Rally: XBI Up 40% YoY, Analysts Say Biggest Gains Still Ahead
description: XBI returned 40.3% over the past year while IBB gained 22.2%. Goldman Sachs and Mizuho project continued growth in 2026, driven by rate cuts, record M&A (over $65B in 2025), and Big Pharma racing to fill $300B+ in patent expirations through 2030. Clinical-stage biotech ETFs BBC (+63.7%) and SBIO (+55.1%) led all sectors in 2025.

title: Longevity Investing Crosses the Mainstream: $8.49B Deployed in 2025, Private Capital Doubles
description: Private longevity investment more than doubled in 2025 to $8.49B across 325 deals. The FDA approved the first lifespan-extension drug (Loyal's LOY-002 for dogs) and formally recognized lifespan extension as a valid clinical endpoint. GLP-1 drugs already generating blockbuster revenue now show longevity signals. With Q1 2026 at $3.74B, full-year projections converge on $8-9B.

title: Rubedo's RLS-1496 Shows Positive Phase 1 Results -- First Senolytic GPX4 Modulator in Humans
description: Rubedo Life Sciences reported preliminary Phase 1 data for RLS-1496, the first GPX4 modulator tested in humans. The topical senolytic was well-tolerated across all doses, showed clear dose-response target engagement, reduced senescent cells in mid- and high-dose cohorts, and achieved a 20% reduction in epidermal thickness after just 4 weeks. A systemic oral formulation is planned for Phase 1 in 2026.

title: Longevity Biotech Funding Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026 -- 56% Above Q1 2025
description: Data from the DLT platform shows 49 financing events in Q1 2026 totaling $3.74 billion, a 56% increase over Q1 2025's $2.40B. The median deal size of $21.8M signals a maturing pipeline graduating beyond seed stage. Full-year 2026 is projected at $8-9B, representing 55-60% growth over 2025.

title: Laromestrocel Stem Cell Therapy Reverses Frailty in Phase 2b -- 63m Walk Test Gain
description: Longeveron's Phase 2b trial of laromestrocel in 148 frail older adults showed a 63.4-meter improvement in the 6-minute walk test at 9 months versus placebo (p=0.0077). Nearly one-third of treated patients improved to non-frail status. Published in Cell Stem Cell, these are the first Phase 2b results for a stem cell therapy in aging frailty.

title: BioAge Labs BGE-102 Achieves 86% hsCRP Reduction -- Oral NLRP3 Inhibitor Advances to Phase 2a
description: BioAge's oral, brain-penetrant NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 delivered potential best-in-class results in Phase 1: 86% median hsCRP reduction, 58% IL-6 reduction, and 30% fibrinogen reduction at Day 14. Phase 2a trials for cardiovascular risk and diabetic macular edema are both planned for H1 2026, backed by a $132.3M upsized offering.

title: 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity Opens in Berlin -- Aging as Loss of Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity convenes April 8-9 in Berlin, shifting the scientific debate from 'fixing aging' to preserving biological coordination. Researchers across mitochondria, microbiota, redox biology, and systems medicine argue aging may be a progressive loss of inter-system communication rather than a single-target defect.

title: Rubedo RLS-1496 Phase 1: First GPX4 Modulator Shows Senescent Cell Clearance in Humans
description: Preliminary results from Rubedo's Phase 1 trial show RLS-1496 was well-tolerated, demonstrated dose-dependent GPX4 target engagement, reduced senescent cells, and lowered inflammatory cytokines. A 20% reduction in epidermal thickness was observed after one month of treatment.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment: $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT data shows Q1 2026 was one of the most active quarterly windows in longevity biotech, with $3.74B raised across 49 financing events. Full-year projections converge on $8-9B, up 55-60% from 2025's $5.72B base.

title: Stem Cell Therapy Reverses Aging Frailty in Phase 2b Trial
description: Longeveron's laromestrocel, a bone-marrow-derived MSC therapy, improved 6-minute walk distance by 63.4 meters at 9 months in 148 frail adults aged 70-85 (p=0.0077). Nearly 31% of treated patients improved to non-frail status.

title: BioAge Labs BGE-102 Phase 1: 86% hsCRP Reduction with Oral NLRP3 Inhibitor
description: BioAge's oral NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 achieved 86% hsCRP reduction and 58% IL-6 reduction at Day 14 in participants with obesity. Phase 2a trials in CV risk and diabetic macular edema planned for H1 2026. Company raised $132.3M in upsized offering.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Aging as Systems Coordination Failure
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity (Berlin, April 8-9) reframes aging as progressive loss of coordination between metabolism, immunity, mitochondria, and microbial ecosystems rather than a single-target defect.

title: Fight Aging Rally: Multi-City Movement for Life Extension on April 8
description: Organizers are planning a multi-city demonstration on April 8, 2026 to bring life extension advocacy into the mainstream, coinciding with the Berlin longevity congress.

title: Buck Institute Launches Healthspan Horizons Federated Data Platform
description: The Buck Institute unveiled Healthspan Horizons, a federated research platform linking wearable, sleep, nutrition, and lab data with deep biological discovery to create computable healthspan trajectories and earlier disease risk signals.

title: Harvard and Yonsei Researchers Claim Reverse Aging Possible, Trials Imminent
description: David Sinclair stated a team of 20+ Harvard researchers has identified root causes of aging, making it possible to overcome aging. Clinical trials of reverse aging on humans will begin soon, with animal experiments already showing aged tissue rejuvenation.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Major Multi-Drug Aging Trial
description: Over 700 South Texans will participate in one of the largest human trials testing whether rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin can slow biological aging. Initial rapamycin dosing sub-study enrolling 84 adults aged 65-90.

title: Life Biosciences Begins First Human Partial Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, received FDA IND clearance to test partial epigenetic reprogramming (OSK Yamanaka factors) in patients with glaucoma and NAION. First patients to enroll within months, with results expected by year-end.

title: Top Longevity Startups: Over $13B in Total Venture Funding
description: Altos Labs leads at $3B, followed by Oura and Retro Biosciences at $1.2B each. Retro's $1B Series A is the largest single longevity round. Top 10 startups capture about 67% of total funding across the sector.

title: Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Narrowing Toward Precision
description: A Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding ABT263 and ARV825 most effective. While early human trials show positive biological signals, clear efficacy evidence is still lacking. The field is shifting toward more selective, biomarker-driven approaches.

title: PEP Acts as Immune Checkpoint to Reduce Chronic Inflammation in Aging
description: A Nature Aging 2026 study found that PEP functions as a metabolic 'brake' that reduces chronic inflammation associated with aging, suggesting a new immune checkpoint mechanism for slowing biological decline.

title: Longevity World Forum: Targeting Aging Itself May Yield Larger Gains Than Single Diseases
description: At the 2026 Longevity World Forum, experts argued that curing a single major disease may only add a couple of years to lifespan, while addressing the biological processes of aging could unlock much larger longevity gains.

title: Fight Aging: Exerkines, Physical Activity Patterns, and Ovarian Aging Updates
description: This week's Fight Aging roundup covers exerkine research on exercise-induced exosomes, a study showing consistent physical activity halves mortality risk, cGAS-STING pathway's role in ovarian aging, and oral microbiome links to cognitive decline.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Key players include Altos Labs ($3B), Life Biosciences (FDA-cleared reprogramming trial), NewLimit (epigenetic reprogramming), Cambrian Bio ($30.8M ARPA-H award), Rubedo Life Sciences (RLS-1496 Phase 1), and Junevity (siRNA cell reset).

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $23.2B, Projected to Reach $34.8B by 2030
description: The longevity biotech market is growing at a 10.7% CAGR, projected from $23.2B in 2026 to $34.82B by 2030, driven by cellular therapies, AI drug discovery, and the convergence of metabolic and aging biology.

title: GLP-1 Drugs Could Reduce US All-Cause Mortality by 6.4% by 2045
description: A Swiss Re model estimates GLP-1 agonists could reduce all-cause US mortality by 6.4% by 2045. The GLP-1 market is projected to grow from $55B to $150B by 2030, with researchers exploring roles in brain health and longevity beyond metabolic benefits.

title: Leading Aging Researchers Predict First FDA-Approved Anti-Aging Drug in Animals
description: Gladyshev, Gorbunova, Sinclair, and other top researchers predict 2026 will bring organ-resolved aging maps, routine biological age monitoring, first antiaging interventions in clinical practice, and the first FDA approval for a drug that slows aging in companion animals.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Projected to Surpass $420 Billion by 2030
description: The global anti-aging therapy market is projected to surpass $420B by 2030 at 8-9% CAGR, driven by cellular therapies and regenerative medicine moving beyond supplements into pharmaceutical solutions.

title: Rapamycin Review: PEARL Trial Shows Tolerability but Long-Term Benefits Unclear
description: Comprehensive review finds low-dose intermittent rapamycin was well tolerated over one year in the PEARL trial with modest biomarker changes, but long-term clinical benefits remain unestablished. Timing and dosing strategy are critical variables.

title: David Sinclair 2025 Protocol: Drops Taurine and TMG, Switches to Berberine
description: Sinclair's updated stack removes taurine and TMG, replaces daily metformin with berberine for AMPK activation, and reduces rapamycin to just 4 times per year based on epigenetic clock data showing limited effect. NMN remains the cornerstone.

title: Peter Attia 2026 Supplement Protocol: Rapamycin, SGLT2i, and Aggressive CV Risk Management
description: Attia's current stack includes rapamycin for longevity, an SGLT2 inhibitor, aggressive lipid management (ezetimibe, Repatha, bempedoic acid), plus omega-3 (2.5g EPA + 1g DHA), vitamin D, magnesium, and pregabalin/trazodone for sleep architecture.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Key Research Highlights
description: Highlights include vitamins C and B2 associated with slower biological aging, mitochondrial stress as key to senolytic effectiveness, partial reprogramming rescuing memory-encoding neurons, and resistance exercise decelerating brain aging clocks.

title: Switzerland Emerges as Longevity Hub with CHF 2.5B Biotech Investment
description: Switzerland boasts one of the world's highest life expectancies at 84 years, with Ticino region reaching 84.6. BioInnovation Day at CHUV (April 1) showcased 29 breakthrough life sciences projects in biotech, medtech, and digital health.
